hazards.description: Late or missing sample delivery under back-pressure
hazards.situation: High-rate publisher overwhelms a slow subscriber; back-pressure policy drops samples before they are consumed
hazards.mitigation: BackPressurePolicy enforced per REQ-ASIL-008; DropNewest/DropOldest/Block configurable per QoS; drop visibility tracked per REQ-IEC-007

hazards.description: Corrupt or truncated payload delivered to subscriber
hazards.situation: Internal serialisation fault or memory corruption produces a malformed payload reaching the application
hazards.mitigation: All payloads are opaque Vec<u8>; transport is in-process (MockParticipant) with no serialisation step; future RTPS transport must add CRC or TLS

hazards.description: Sample delivered to wrong subscriber (topic isolation failure)
hazards.situation: Broker routing table corrupted or topic string comparison fails; sample from topic A reaches subscriber on topic B
hazards.mitigation: Broker uses exact HashMap key match on topic string per REQ-IEC-006; Mutex prevents concurrent map mutation; tested by multi-topic isolation tests per REQ-DO-008

hazards.situation: Participant created on domain D1 receives messages published on domain D2 due to missing domain isolation
hazards.mitigation: Each MockParticipant holds its own Broker instance; no shared broker across domains per REQ-PART-001; domain validated [0,232] per REQ-ASIL-006

hazards.situation: Application calls unsubscribe() then awaits recv(); recv() never returns None if channel is not closed
hazards.mitigation: unsubscribe() calls close() on SubInner per §6.4 fix; recv() drains and returns None; regression test unsubscribe_closes_channel_so_recv_returns_none

hazards.description: Sequence number wraparound produces false ordering
hazards.situation: u64 sequence counter wraps around to 0 after 2^64 writes; application interprets wrap as a very old sample
hazards.mitigation: u64 provides ~1.8×10^19 writes before wrap; at 1M writes/s this is >580,000 years; documented per REQ-ASIL-005

hazards.description: Write after close returns success instead of error
hazards.situation: Publisher or Participant is closed; subsequent write() call proceeds and delivers data to subscribers still open on the topic
hazards.mitigation: AtomicBool closed flag checked at entry of every write() and send(); returns Error::Closed per REQ-ASIL-007 and §6.1–6.2

hazards.description: GUID collision causes wrong writer attribution on received samples
hazards.situation: Two publishers on the same participant receive the same GUID prefix + counter; downstream code uses writer_guid for security decisions
hazards.mitigation: GUID prefix encodes domain in byte 0, AtomicU64 pub_counter (fetch_add) in bytes 8-15; no two publishers on the same participant share a GUID per REQ-SEC-007

hazards.description: Empty or malicious topic name causes undefined routing
hazards.situation: Caller passes an empty string or path-traversal string as topic; broker creates an entry that can never be matched or masks an existing topic
hazards.mitigation: Topic emptiness validated at new_publisher/new_subscriber entry; returns Error::TopicEmpty per REQ-SEC-001 and REQ-SEC-013

hazards.description: Payload exceeds safe size bound causing memory exhaustion
hazards.situation: Caller writes an unbounded payload; allocation fails or OOM kills the process in a safety-critical embedded context
hazards.mitigation: MockParticipant imposes no payload size limit (future RTPS transport must gate on REQ-SEC-002 PayloadTooLarge); current ASIL-C risk accepted for mock-only transport milestone
